mapping.a_element: gauged linear sigma models (GLSMs) describing geometric structures (e.g., partial flag manifolds, Calabi-Yau complete intersections)
mapping.b_element: nonlinear elliptic PDEs (Witten equation) describing moduli spaces of solutions in singularity/Landau-Ginzburg theory

mapping.a_element: Kahler phases and their non-birational relationships (e.g., homological projective duality)
mapping.b_element: wall-crossing phenomena in the deformation of virtual cycles under perturbation

mapping.a_element: moduli spaces realized as Quot and hyperquot schemes in GLSMs
mapping.b_element: virtual fundamental cycle and extended virtual cycle for the moduli space of solutions to the Witten equation

mapping.a_element: physical realization of geometric structures via GLSMs
mapping.b_element: construction of virtual cycles satisfying axioms analogous to Gromov-Witten theory

brief_justification: While both papers involve moduli spaces and phase-like phenomena (Kahler phases in A, wall-crossing in B), their underlying machinery differs fundamentally: A uses gauged linear sigma models to realize geometric structures, whereas B constructs virtual cycles for solutions to nonlinear elliptic PDEs. The shared terms (e.g., 'moduli spaces') refer to distinct mathematical objects and methods, and no role-for-role correspondence in the core mechanisms (e.g., GLSM dynamics vs. PDE analysis) is evident.
